articles.title: EARLY-STAGE STARTUPS
articles.summary: Vibe-coding your homepage is the easiest way to look exactly like everyone else. With Framer [4], hundreds of YC-backed startups have built polished and unique sites in hours, not weeks.

articles.title: 👉 Apply to claim your free year
articles.summary: (Pre-seed and seed-stage startups new to Framer are eligible.)

articles.title: CHINESE AI DEVELOPERS SAY THEY CAN'T BEAT AMERICA WITHOUT BETTER CHIPS
articles.summary: Some elite Chinese AI researchers estimate that the country's chances of catching up to the US are slim in the short run due to a bottleneck in chips. US rules block the direct sale of Nvidia chips to China. Chinese companies can rent Nvidia chips in third countries, but this typically leaves developers with fewer chips and more inconvenience compared with well-funded competitors in the US. The US recently unblocked sales of Nvidia's H200 chip to China, but this is not likely to be a game-changer in helping Chinese companies catch up.

articles.title: MOBILE APPS ON REPLIT
articles.summary: Replit launched a new AI-powered coding assistant designed to enhance coding efficiency and collaboration. This tool integrates seamlessly into its platform, providing real-time code suggestions and intelligent code completion. It aims to streamline the coding process for developers and teams.

articles.title: CLAUDE ECONOMIC INDEX REPORT
articles.summary: Anthropic published its January 2026 Economic Index, offering new data on how Claude is used across geographies and sectors. The report introduces five metrics to track Claude's economic footprint and includes the most detailed usage dataset released to date.

articles.title: NVIDIA'S ACHILLES HEEL: INFERENCE
articles.summary: Benchmark's Eric Vishria shares his email to investors about Nvidia from 2024. Nvidia's dominance holds in training, but GenAI inference is emerging as the weak spot. Purpose-built SRAM architectures from players like Groq and Cerebras deliver order-of-magnitude faster inference than GPUs, unlocking larger models, lower latency, and new use cases. As inference becomes a growing share of spend, performance gaps could shift AI compute winners away from incumbents.

articles.summary: Turn any knowledge base into an AI that people trust. Scroll.ai [9]'s platform provides accuracy and context-adherence that generic models can't match. articles.title: ON NEURAL SCALING AND THE QUANTA HYPOTHESIS
articles.summary: Humanity started an experiment in scaling up deep neural networks several years ago. The experiment is on track to be the most expensive ever attempted, and we don't yet know what the results of the experiment will be. Despite its importance, there isn't a mature theory backing the experiment. We don't really know how to think about what neural networks are doing internally. The number of people working on such a theory in public is relatively small.

articles.title: GOOGLE'S TRANSLATEGEMMA
articles.summary: Google's TranslateGemma is a set of open machine translation models built on Gemma 3 and fine-tuned using synthetic and human-translated data, followed by reinforcement learning with quality-focused reward models. These models outperform Gemma 3 baselines across 55 language pairs and retain strong multimodal capabilities.

articles.title: SEQUENCE DISTILLATION FOR EFFICIENT REASONING
articles.summary: DASD is a distillation pipeline that combines techniques like temperature-scheduled learning and divergence-aware sampling to train compact models for reasoning tasks. Its 4B and 30B variants achieve strong results on coding, math, and science benchmarks.

articles.title: NVIDIA SPEEDS UP AI REASONING WITH FAST-THINKACT
HOW SCIENTISTS ARE USING CLAUDE CODE?
articles.summary: Anthropic detailed progress on Claude for Life Sciences and its AI for Science program, highlighting Claude's expanding role as a research collaborator. The update showcases Opus 4.5's improvements in scientific reasoning and new use cases across labs accelerating discovery through AI-powered experimentation and analysis.

articles.title: AI IS EVERYWHERE, BUT NOWHERE IN RECENT PRODUCTIVITY DATA
articles.summary: Forrester's J.P. Gownder argues that AI hasn't yet boosted productivity, similar to how PCs didn't immediately impact productivity metrics. His research suggests AI could replace 6% of jobs by 2030, with many companies yet to see tangible ROI from AI projects. He contrasts AI's slow job-replacement with previous shifts like outsourcing, highlighting financial decisions rather than direct AI replacements driving current job losses.

articles.title: TWO COFOUNDERS LEAVE THINKING MACHINES LAB TO RETURN TO OPENAI
articles.summary: Thinking Machines' cofounders, Barret Zoph and Luke Metz, along with Sam Schoenholz, are returning to OpenAI.

articles.title: APPLE IS FIGHTING FOR TSMC CAPACITY AS NVIDIA TAKES CENTER STAGE
articles.summary: Apple faces competition from Nvidia for TSMC's chip production capacity, as Nvidia's demand for AI chips rises with the tech boom.

articles.title: INVESTING IN MERGE LABS
articles.summary: OpenAI has invested in Merge Labs, a research lab with the long-term mission of bridging biological and artificial intelligence to maximize human ability, agency, and experience.
